questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The mass number of an atom is the sum of the number of protons and neutrons present in its nucleus.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The mass number is denoted by A and it gives the total number of nucleons in the nucleus.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The mass number is the sum of protons and neutrons, denoted by A, which gives the total number of nucleons in the nucleus.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Isotopes have the same atomic number but different mass numbers.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Isotopes are atoms of the same element having the same number of protons (atomic number) but different number of neutrons.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Isotopes have the same atomic number (number of protons) but different mass numbers due to variations in the number of neutrons.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The binding energy per nucleon increases with an increase in the mass number.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The binding energy per nucleon is the energy required to remove a nucleon from the nucleus, which generally increases with an increase in mass number.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The binding energy per nucleon increases with an increase in mass number due to the increase in the energy required to remove a nucleon from the nucleus.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Alpha decay is a type of radioactive decay in which an alpha particle is emitted from the nucleus.
questions.reason: Reason (R): An alpha particle is a helium nucleus consisting of two protons and two neutrons, which is emitted during alpha decay.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Alpha decay involves the emission of an alpha particle from the nucleus, which is a helium nucleus consisting of two protons and two neutrons.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Beta decay is a type of radioactive decay in which a beta particle is emitted from the nucleus.
questions.reason: Reason (R): A beta particle is either a positron or a negatively charged electron, which is emitted during beta decay.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Beta decay involves the emission of a beta particle from the nucleus, which is either a positron or a negatively charged electron.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Nuclear fission is a process in which a heavy nucleus splits into two or more lighter nuclei.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Nuclear fission is a process in which an atomic nucleus splits to form two or more lighter nuclei, along with the release of a large amount of energy.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Nuclear fission is a process in which a heavy nucleus splits into two or more lighter nuclei, releasing a large amount of energy in the process.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The half-life of a radioactive substance is the time required for half of the initial amount of the substance to decay.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The half-life is a characteristic property of a radioactive substance, which depends on the type of radioactive decay and the stability of the nucleus.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The half-life of a radioactive substance is the time required for half of the initial amount of the substance to decay, which is a characteristic property of the substance.

(C) A is true but R is false

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Radioactive decay is a spontaneous process in which a nucleus emits radiation and transforms into a more stable nucleus.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Radioactive decay occurs due to the imbalance of protons and neutrons in the nucleus, leading to the emission of radiation and transformation into a more stable nucleus.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Radioactive decay is a spontaneous process in which a nucleus emits radiation and transforms into a more stable nucleus due to the imbalance of protons and neutrons.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The atomic mass unit (amu) is a unit of mass that is equal to one-twelfth the mass of a carbon-12 atom.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The amu is a standard unit of mass that is used to express the mass of atoms and molecules, and it is defined as one-twelfth the mass of a carbon-12 atom.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
